must_detect.matched_text:
Strictly Confidential — MNPI — Operational/Cyber Incident Report for Siraj Oasis Utilities PJSC (DIFC branch) — Project Palm Dune
On 03 Jun 2026 02:10 GST, credentials of a facilities HVAC subcontractor were abused, causing a 27‑minute outage on the ADGM data mirror and potential read‑only access to limited HR and vendor folders.
Potentially impacted PDPL data categories include Emirates ID scans, onshore trade licence attachments for contractors, user emails, and voluntary family-origin survey responses used for ESG reporting.
This occurred while negotiating a definitive Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) under Project Palm Dune to acquire 12.5% of Shamal Radiant Holdings LLC for AED 420,000,000.
Embargoed until 18 Jul 2026 10:00 GST; no external communications without CFO and Company Secretary approval; IR list restricted.
Preliminary assessment: this does not constitute a Material Adverse Change (MAC) or MAE under the SPA, subject to bring-down at signing.
We will brief the SCA and, for DIFC operations, the DFSA; ADGM FSRA notification threshold appears unmet given <0.5% revenue impact and zero safety incidents.
Our 2024 sustainability report, publicly available on sirajoasis.ae, already outlines HVAC network segmentation and vendor risk management.
Under UAE PDPL, data subjects will be notified if forensic analysis confirms access to identified or indirectly identifiable personal data.
Board briefing with Mr. Sami Al Qasimi (Chair) and Ms. Ranya Dabbagh (Audit Committee) set for 05 Jun 2026; IR to prepare draft Q&A for potential ADX inquiries.
Deal team to confirm whether SPA reps on cybersecurity require a specific indemnity or disclosure schedule update; MAC carve-outs to be reconfirmed.
Contact: Dr. Nabil Faroukh, CISO, +971-00-0000-1234, ciso@sirajoasis.ae; alternate: esg@sirajoasis.ae.
